definitions: Describing a relationship between two genes or two agents (e.g., a gene and a drug) where the individual inactivation of either gene or application of either agent has no effect on cell viability, but the simultaneous inactivation of both genes or application of both agents leads to cell death.
Pertaining to a genetic interaction where the combination of two individually non-lethal mutations results in lethality.
examples: The researchers identified a synthetic lethal interaction between gene A and gene B.
Targeting synthetic lethal vulnerabilities is a promising strategy in cancer therapy.
The drug exhibits synthetic lethal activity when combined with a specific genetic mutation.
